items.title: Melaka PH to decide position on state govt after DAP pullout
items.summary: AYER KEROH: Melaka Pakatan Harapan will decide its future direction in the state government tomorrow following DAP's decision to quit the Barisan Nasional-led administration. PH chairman Adly Zahari said the views of all assemblymen and component parties within the coalition would be considered before a final decision is announced. "They (the four DAP assemblymen) have informed us of their decision (withdrawing support).

items.title: LRT, MRT disruptions drop to 12 in first five months - Loke
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Disruptions on the LRT and MRT networks have shown a declining trend, falling from 177 incidents in 2022 to 28 last year and 12 in the first five months of this year, Transport Minister Anthony Loke said. Loke said the Mean Kilometres Between Failures (MKBF) had reached 930,000km across all LRT and MRT lines as of May 31, compared with just 90,000km in 2022. "The latest MKBF achievement as of May 31, 2026, shows an improving trend in overall performance compared with previous years," Loke said.

items.title: US expands sanctions targeting Iran oil, cryptocurrency sectors
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The US on Tuesday expanded its sanctions targeting Iran's oil sector, taking further aim at the network of petroleum shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, the Treasury Department said. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the department had also frozen US$130 million held in digital wallets linked to Iran's central bank, hitting a sector that has seen increased activity since the start of the war. Iran started blocking the strait – a key waterway for energy transit – after US-Israel attacks in February.

items.title: Anwar: Israel nationals found in Malaysia will be deported immediately
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Malaysia will not compromise and will deport any Israeli nationals found entering the country, as it has a clear policy of not recognising Israel, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said all relevant security agencies were conducting a thorough investigation into allegations that Israeli nationals had been detected in Malaysia. He was commenting on media reports alleging that Israeli nationals using dual citizenship documents had been found in Johor.

items.title: King grants audience to Johor MB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, today granted an audience to Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi in Johor Bahru. According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar's official Facebook page, Onn Hafiz briefed His Majesty on several matters, including the Johor government's administration following the recently concluded 16th Johor election, as well as the state's administrative direction and development plans. In Saturday's election, Barisan Nasional (BN) retained its mandate to govern Johor after winning 48 of the 56 seats in the Johor State Legislative Assembly, enabling it to form the government with a larger majority.

items.title: No need to review Budget 2027 projections despite higher fuel subsidy bill, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — There is no need to review current projections under Budget 2027 based on the government’s fiscal performance for the first half of 2026, said Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong. However, he said the government would continue to monitor current developments until Budget 2027 is tabled in October. He said higher oil prices had increased government subsidy expenditure by an estimated RM40 billion.

items.title: Sabah seeks Budi Diesel revamp as state highlights burden on farmers, rural communities
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 15 — Sabah expects revisions and improvements to the BUDI targeted diesel subsidy scheme within the next two to three weeks following engagement sessions between the state government and relevant federal ministries. State Agriculture, Fisheries and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the issue was raised at a recent State Cabinet meeting after discussions with the Finance Ministry and the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) on the subsidy scheme’s impact on Sabahans. Jamawi said the first session will be held on Friday at Menara Kinabalu with heads of state departments, while a second session involving government assemblymen will take place on Sunday.

items.title: Forest City to enforce legal rights if Network School breached laws
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Forest City would enforce its full legal and contractual rights if investigations found Network School had breached any Malaysian law, immigration, licensing, land-use or contractual requirements. The township's developer, Country Garden Pacificview Sdn Bhd, said it would extend its cooperation to authorities investigating allegations linked to Network School, including issues surrounding an individual's nationality. "Should the authorities establish any legal, immigration, licensing, land-use or contractual breach, Forest City will enforce its full legal and contractual rights," it said in a statement today.

items.title: Woman abandoned as infant not entitled to citizenship by operation of law, says Court of Appeal
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A woman found abandoned at a bus station when she was about one year old is not entitled to Malaysian citizenship by operation of law, the Court of Appeal ruled recently. A three-member panel comprising Datuk Azhahari Kamal Ramli, Datuk Faizah Jamaludin and Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz held that the constitutional presumption of citizenship applies only to "a newborn child found exposed" and not to children abandoned after infancy. "The difficulty is that Section 19B is confined to a newborn child found exposed.

items.title: [UPDATED] 'We'll chase you out,' Anwar warns Israelis at Network School
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said the government would expel any Israeli nationals found to be involved in the Network School, a co-living and co-working community in Forest City, Johor. He said the matter was being investigated by the relevant authorities. Yesterday, the Home Ministry said it had launched an investigation into the operations of Network School following allegations that some individuals had entered Malaysia using second-country passports.

items.title: More than 3,500 medical officers quit public healthcare between 2023 and 2025, says Health Ministry
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — More than 3,500 medical officers left Malaysia’s public healthcare sector over a three-year period ending in 2025, adding pressure on a state medical system already struggling with workforce shortages and staff burnout. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad yesterday said in a written parliamentary reply exactly 3,511 medical officers resigned from the Ministry of Health between 2023 and 2025. “The Ministry of Health doesn’t have any legal provision to prevent medical officers from seeking employment in the private sector, including preventing recruitment efforts undertaken by the private sector or registered foreign talent recruitment agencies,” he said.

items.title: PAC welcomes Felcra governance improvements, says adherence must be tested
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The effectiveness of measures to improve governance at the Federal Land Consolidation and Rehabilitation Authority (Felcra) has yet to be tested, said the Public Accounts Committee (PAC). PAC chairman Datuk Mas Ermieyati Samsudin said the committee noted the governance improvements made by Felcra, which include requiring an external consultant's viability assessment to be presented to the board of directors before any purchase is made. On financial projections, the committee welcomed Felcra's revised return-on-investment calculations for its four troubled estates — Telupid, Dabong, Sungai Rawit 2 and Aring.

items.title: Organised crime evolves into sophisticated business, says MCBA chief
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Organised crime has evolved beyond conventional illegal activity into a sophisticated business enterprise driven by technology, global networks and professional expertise. Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said modern organised crime syndicates had developed their own ecosystems, enabling them to expand systematically across geographical and national borders. Crime is a business entity.

items.title: MCBA: Inspection dispute leads to shooting threat against officer
items.summary: MCBA director-general Datuk Seri Mohd Shuhaily Mohd Zain said preliminary checks found that the incident occurred after the suspect, believed to be an agent, allegedly failed to cooperate with officers during the initial stage of an inspection at Port Klang. He said the MCBA officer was merely carrying out his duties in accordance with standard operating procedures before a misunderstanding arose with the suspect. Port Klang MCBA commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said a police report had been lodged at the Klang Selatan district police headquarters following the incident.

items.title: Heritage conservation needs corporate support, says minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Companies should do their part in heritage conservation and development, National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang said, urging more public-private collaborations. He said heritage development should not rely solely on government funding, citing sustainable models used in other countries. He said the RM5 million Museum Matching Grant should be leveraged to expand these partnerships.

items.title: Aaron wants national database to link museum artefacts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang has urged the Department of Museums Malaysia to establish a National Artefact Collection Database to consolidate information from museums nationwide. He said Malaysia should emulate countries such as Norway and Sweden, which connect hundreds of museums through digitised systems for public access. Director-general of the Department of Museums Malaysia Kamarul Baharin A. Kasim said the first immersive exhibition would be housed at Gallery D of the National Museum in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Malaysia's Q2 GDP growth expected to moderate: economists
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's second quarter (2Q 2026) gross domestic product (GDP) growth is expected to moderate to 4.8-5.0 per cent from 5.4 per cent in 1Q 2026. IPP Financial Advisers (IPPFA) director of investment strategy and country economist, Mohd Sedek Jantan said Malaysia's domestic economy remains resilient, despite heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and renewed volatility in global energy prices. "We believe stronger domestic demand, resilient external trade and a supportive policy environment more than offset external headwinds, allowing economic activity to remain on a solid expansion path," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Immigration D-G: All 266 foreigners inspected at Forest City had valid documents, further checks underway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — All 266 foreign nationals inspected at an international community in Forest City, Johor, were found to possess valid immigration documents based on current records, the Immigration Department said today. “Preliminary checks found that all individuals inspected possessed valid immigration documents based on current records. “The Immigration Department will not compromise on any breach of immigration law or misuse of immigration facilities,” he said.

items.title: Liew Chin Tong: No subsidy cuts planned despite soaring petroleum costs linked to West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The government will not reduce any existing subsidies to the people despite being projected to bear almost RM40 billion in petroleum product subsidies this year due to the conflict in West Asia. Deputy Finance Minister Liew Chin Tong said the government’s current stance is to continue various forms of assistance, including Sumbangan Tunai Rahmah (STR) and Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA). Liew said the government has ensured stability in petrol prices and fuel supply despite facing global market uncertainties.

items.title: Get a physical driving licence if you're driving to Thailand, RTD urges public
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has urged Malaysians driving into Thailand to obtain a physical driving licence to avoid being fined by Thai authorities. Kelantan RTD director Mohd Misuari Abdullah said digital licences were sufficient for driving in Malaysia, but physical licences were mandatory in Thailand. He said a physical licence costs RM20.

items.title: MMA wants healthcare workforce dashboard to drive reforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for a comprehensive healthcare workforce dashboard to complement the Health Ministry's newly granted autonomy over workforce management, saying it is essential to improve transparency and support evidence-based workforce planning. The association also welcomed the offer of permanent appointments to 4,500 contract medical officers, the establishment of the Health Ministry-Public Service Department (PSD) Workforce Management Task Force, closer collaboration with the Higher Education Ministry on long-term workforce planning, and the introduction of relocation benefits for eligible medical officers. "Greater transparency will strengthen public confidence, facilitate better workforce planning and enable more timely interventions to address healthcare workforce challenges," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Melaka allocates RM5mil for immediate flood repair works
items.summary: MELAKA: The Melaka government has approved an immediate allocation of RM5 million to carry out repair and restoration works on drainage systems and infrastructure damaged by the floods that hit the state on Saturday. State Senior Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin said the decision was made during the Post-Flood Coordination Meeting he chaired on Monday. Earlier, Datuk Siti Faizah Abdul Azis (BN-Sungai Rambai) asked whether the state government planned to implement flood mitigation projects in Sungai Rambai, which frequently experiences flash floods.

items.title: Sofia Rini charged with defaming businessman Farhash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A 50-year-old woman pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court today to a charge of defaming businessman Datuk Seri Farhash Wafa Salvador Rizal Mubarak. "I claim trial," she said calmly, shaking her head. Fadhly asked the court to set bail at RM15,000 with one surety.

items.title: Melaka sets aside RM5 million for urgent flood repairs after Saturday's flash floods
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — The Melaka government has approved an immediate allocation of RM5 million to carry out repair and restoration works on drainage systems and infrastructure damaged during the floods that hit the state on Saturday (July 11). State senior Housing, Local Government, Drainage, Climate Change and Disaster Management Committee chairman Datuk Rais Yasin said this was decided during the Post-Flood Coordination Meeting that he chaired on July 13. Earlier, Datuk Siti Faizah Abdul Azis (BN-Sungai Rambai) asked if the state government had plans to implement flood mitigation projects in the Sungai Rambai area, which frequently experiences flash floods.

items.title: Petaling Jaya MP says Bestinet served him legal notice over foreign worker system remarks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Petaling Jaya MP Lee Chean Chung said today he has been served with a letter of demand (LOD) by Bestinet Sdn Bhd following a series of parliamentary questions and public statements he made regarding the Foreign Worker Centralised Management System (FWCMS) and the company. “As a Member of Parliament, I have a duty to represent the people by exercising oversight over the government, raising questions, and seeking accountability on matters involving significant public interest,” Lee said. Lee said the legal letter was also addressed to The Edge Communications Sdn Bhd.

items.title: Ahmad Badri appointed Felda chairman, succeeds Ahmad Shabery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Land Development Authority (Felda) has appointed Tan Sri Ahmad Badri Mohd Zahir as its chairman, succeeding Datuk Seri Ahmad Shabery Cheek, whose three-year tenure ended on June 30. In a statement, Felda said the appointment reflects the government's confidence in Ahmad Badri's leadership, integrity and extensive experience in leading strategic organisations. Ahmad Shabery was appointed Felda chairman on July 1, 2023.

items.title: Thai authorities arrest three over civil service exam rigging scandal
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thai authorities have arrested three key suspects over a nationwide civil service examination rigging scandal that could cost about 5,000 officials their jobs. He said the investigation would uncover the full extent of the scheme and that those involved would be brought to justice. He said everyone found to have been involved in manipulating the examination process would face legal action.

items.title: Court-ordered compensation to road crash victims, family a strong message
items.summary: LETTERS: I welcome the Transport Ministry's proposal to empower the courts to order irresponsible and reckless drivers convicted of serious road traffic offences to pay compensation to victims or, in cases involving fatalities, to their lawful next of kin. Road crashes often leave victims and their families with enormous physical, emotional and financial burdens. A court-ordered compensation mechanism recognises that those whose reckless actions cause serious harm should bear a greater measure of responsibility for the consequences of their conduct.

items.title: Ramanan says RM12.54m allocated to 627 Hindu temples as Madani initiatives continue
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 15 — The government has allocated RM12.54 million to 627 Hindu temples approved under the Dharma Madani programme between January and July this year, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said. The first phase of the programme, he said, saw 162 Hindu temples receive RM3.24 million in funding on Jan 27, and a further 297 temples benefited under the second phase, with RM5.94 million disbursed on April 28. Ramanan said the Madani Government, through the Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (MITRA), is also strengthening educational support for the Indian community through the Early Education Subsidy Assistance Programme (Celik MADANI 2026), with an allocation of RM10 million.

items.title: [UPDATED] All 266 foreigners in Forest City community hold valid papers, says Immigration
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Preliminary checks by the Immigration Department found that all 266 foreigners inspected at a community in Forest City possessed valid immigration documents, although investigations into the controversial "Network School" are ongoing. He said the department would also investigate any new information related to identity fraud, misuse of travel documents or immigration facilities, and breaches of pass conditions. The ministry said it viewed seriously any matter involving national security, compliance with immigration laws and the misuse of Malaysia's immigration facilities.

items.title: Rapid Bus names former Kontena Nasional chief Syahrul Azmir as CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Rapid Bus Sdn Bhd has appointed Syahrul Azmir Ahmad Zaki as its new chief executive officer effective from July 15, 2026. The company's chairman Datuk Seri Mustafar Ali said that Syahrul's appointment comes at a crucial time as the company continues to strengthen the country's public bus network to meet growing mobility demands. "Syahrul's extensive experience in leading large-scale operations and driving organisational transformation will further strengthen Rapid Bus' efforts to improve service reliability, enhance customer experience, and accelerate the company's digitalisation and sustainability agenda.

items.title: 100 boxes of suspected illicit liquor seized, two men held
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 100 boxes of suspected untaxed liquor were seized and two men, including an Indian national, were detained during the Op Taring Alpha 2 Intra/Inter raid on Jalan Berai, Klang, Selangor, yesterday. General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 4 Commanding Officer Superintendent Amanjit Singh said that during the 1pm raid, both suspects, in their 40s, were arrested while transferring boxes from a lorry into a car under suspicious circumstances. The suspects and all seized items were taken to the Klang Selatan Police Headquarters for further investigations under Customs Act 1967, Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: CGS International starts YTL Hospitality REIT coverage with RM1.18 target price
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: CGS International has initiated coverage on YTL Hospitality REIT (YTL REIT) with an "Add" call and a dividend discount model-based target price of RM1.18, citing its defensive income profile, earnings visibility and potential distribution per unit (DPU) growth. "We anticipate the yield spread to narrow further given the REIT's strengthening earnings profile, underpinned by visible master lease income, scheduled rental escalations, and incremental contributions from new assets," Tan said in a note. The research house said investors have underestimated YTL REIT's portfolio of premium hospitality assets located in key tourism and business destinations across Malaysia, Australia and Japan.

items.title: Four Melaka DAP assemblymen moved to opposition bloc in state assembly
items.summary: MELAKA: The seats of four Melaka DAP assemblymen in the State Legislative Assembly were moved to the opposition bloc on the final day of the sitting today. However, the sole Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) assemblyman, Adly Zahari (PH-Bukit Katil), who is also Melaka Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, remained seated with the government bloc, separate from the four DAP assemblymen. Melaka DAP announced its immediate withdrawal from the government after the State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the State Constitution to allow for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.

items.title: Man killed, son critically injured after truck overtakes on double line
items.summary: Zaiful Arimey Zainal Abidin, 33, who was riding a Yamaha motorcycle, died at the scene in the 10.45pm accident. Bentong police chief, Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar, said the accident involved a Toyota Hilux driven by a 43-year-old man and Zaiful's motorcycle. Zaiham said the Hilux driver and his passenger escaped unhurt.

items.title: Police seek Interpol Red Notice after Tamim Dahri skips court date
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) has applied for an Interpol Red Notice to locate and bring back activist Tamim Dahri to face proceedings at the Langkawi Magistrate’s Court over alleged insults against Hinduism. He said PDRM submitted the application to Interpol as part of efforts to trace the suspect and facilitate his return to Malaysia. Saifuddin Nasution said PDRM had obtained an arrest warrant from the Langkawi Magistrate’s Court on May 17, 2026, and subsequently blacklisted Tamim.

items.title: 10 commercial vehicle drivers test positive for drugs in Pahang
items.summary: TEMERLOH: Ten of the 351 commercial vehicle drivers screened by the Pahang Road Transport Department (RTD) during its Integrated Anti-Drug Operation at 10 strategic locations in the state since July 1 have tested positive for drugs. Pahang RTD director Syed Ahmad Khirulanwar Alyahya Syed Abdul Rahman said the operation, now into its 14th day, focused on drivers of commercial, public service and tourism vehicles. "So far, the 10 drug positive cases detected involved heavy vehicle drivers aged between 29 and 55.

items.title: High Court rules strata auction buyers need not inherit previous owners’ arrears
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The High Court has ruled that buyers of strata properties at court-ordered auctions cannot automatically be required to settle maintenance charges and sinking fund arrears incurred by previous owners. After Rajinder became the registered owner, the property’s management corporation demanded that he pay more than RM182,000 in arrears incurred by the company. The management corporation argued that the outstanding charges followed the property and that Rajinder was liable as a “successor-in-title” under the Strata Management Act 2013.

items.title: Malaysia not committed to any nuclear partner yet, says deputy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia remains at the study stage on nuclear energy and has not committed to any partner country despite reports suggesting otherwise, Deputy Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister Datuk Seri Abdul Rahman Mohamad said. Rahman said Malaysia was engaging with multiple countries that had expressed interest in its nuclear programme, including France, Russia and China, but said that no decisions had been made. The deputy minister said the government recognised that nuclear energy played an important role in the energy mix and wanted to ensure the right approach was taken.

items.title: Six foreigners fined RM1,200 each for conducting World Cup betting activities [WATCH]
items.summary: BALIK PULAU: Six foreigners were fined RM1,200 each by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to jointly conducting betting activities on 2026 Fifa World Cup football matches. The accused were Chinese nationals Yang Zhilong, Zhou Bao, Xu Kai and Ma Jiahao, and Myanmar nationals Khaing Moe Nwe and Le Kyar Hkyan, aged between 23 and 31. Magistrate Chia Huey Ting imposed the fine on each accused, with one month's imprisonment in default of payment.

items.title: China's economic growth hits slowest pace in more than three years
items.summary: BEIJING: China's economy grew at its weakest pace in more than three years during the second quarter, data showed Wednesday, missing expectations even as strong exports driven by the global AI boom helped offset trade disruptions caused by the Middle East war. The 4.3 per cent year-on-year expansion reported by the National Bureau of Statistics (NBS) for April-June, was short of the 4.5 per cent forecast in an AFP survey of economists and the slowest growth since the fourth quarter of 2022. The NBS data also showed retail sales grew 1.0 year-on-year in June, beating a Bloomberg forecast of a 0.1 per cent drop.

items.title: Melaka assembly: Four DAP assemblymen move to opposition bloc
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — The seats of four Melaka DAP assemblymen in the State Legislative Assembly were moved to the opposition bloc on the final day of the sitting today. However, the sole Parti Amanah Negara (Amanah) assemblyman, Adly Zahari (PH-Bukit Katil), who is also Melaka Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman, remains seated with the government bloc, separate from the four DAP assemblymen. Melaka DAP announced its immediate withdrawal from the state government after the Melaka State Legislative Assembly passed amendments to the State Constitution to allow for the appointment of nominated assemblymen.

items.title: Could Khairy be BN’s state seat pick in Rembau — and even its MB candidate?
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 15 — Former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin is reportedly being considered as a Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate for a state seat in Rembau in the August 1 Negri Sembilan election. The sources reportedly said the proposal followed Khairy’s role in helping BN secure a decisive victory in the recent Johor state election, particularly by attracting younger voters. BN has said it will contest all 36 seats in the state election.

items.title: Police uncover suspected smuggled liquor worth RM409,818 during raid in Klang, two men detained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — A total of 100 boxes of suspected untaxed liquor were seized and two men, including an Indian national, were detained during the Ops Taring Alpha 2 Intra/Inter raid on Jalan Berai, Klang, Selangor, yesterday. Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) General Operations Force (GOF) Battalion 4 Commanding Officer Supt Amanjit Singh said that during the 1 pm raid, both suspects, in their 40s, were arrested while transferring boxes from a lorry into a car under suspicious circumstances. The suspects and all seized items were taken to the Klang Selatan Police Headquarters for further investigations under Customs Act 1967, Immigration Act 1959/63 and the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Johor adopts new mission-based approach for 2040 plan to drive sustainable state development
items.summary: Johor Town and Country Planning Department (PLANMalaysia Johor) director Abdulhan Abd Lamit said the review aims to ensure the state’s physical and socio-economic development planning remains relevant to current and future needs. He said the RSN Johor 2040 would adopt a ‘Mission-Oriented Policy Approach’, under which key development goals would be identified first before supporting strategies are formulated. The Johor Structure Plan 2020 was gazetted in 2008, while the second-generation Johor Structure Plan 2030 was gazetted in March 2020.

items.title: Miti: RM318.5b of approved manufacturing investments realised since 2023, creating over 210,000 jobs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — A total of 2,688 manufacturing investment projects worth RM318.5 billion approved between 2023 and March 2026 have been realised, creating 210,546 new jobs, Investment, Trade and Industry Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani said today. Of the realised investments, he explained that domestic investment (DI) totalled RM63.3 billion or 19.9 per cent, while foreign investment (FI) amounted to RM255.2 billion or 80.1 per cent. Johari said Penang, Kedah, Selangor, Johor and Negeri Sembilan recorded the highest value of realised manufacturing investments, accounting for 84.3 per cent of the total realised investments during the period.

items.title: University student dies in Tawau crash while heading for practical training
items.summary: TAWAU, July 15 — A 24-year-old Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) Shah Alam student died after the motorcycle he was riding was hit by a four-wheel-drive vehicle here, yesterday, cutting short his plan to undergo practical training at the Tawau District Education Office (PPD). Mazlan Tuwo was pronounced dead at the scene along Jalan Tawau-Bombalai, near Kampung Haji Matahir, at about 7.30am after his Yamaha 135LC collided with a Mitsubishi Triton, said Tawau Acting District Police Chief Supt Wilynton Enchana Watt. He said the impact of the collision sent the car skidding onto the road shoulder and into a drain, where it overturned, while the victim and his motorcycle were flung about five metres to the road shoulder facing Tawau town.

items.title: DAP man questions absence of AirBorneo direct Sibu-Kuching flights
items.summary: SIBU, July 15 — Sarawak Democratic Action Party (DAP) committee member Stanley Chiew has questioned why Sarawak government-owned AirBorneo has yet to introduce direct flights between Sibu and Kuching despite its expansion of domestic and international routes. Welcoming AirBorneo’s announcement of direct flights from Kuching to Kuala Lumpur and Kuching to Singapore this month, Chiew said the Sarawak government should also prioritise improving connectivity within the state, particularly between Sibu and Kuching. He added that major healthcare facilities, including the Sarawak Heart Centre and the under-construction Sarawak Cancer Centre in Kota Samarahan, are located in Kuching, making convenient air connectivity essential for residents of central Sarawak.

items.title: Islamic inheritance, Native Courts among nine Bills for Sabah assembly sitting from July 20
items.summary: State Assembly Speaker Datuk Seri Panglima Kadzim M Yahya said the Bills comprise three new Bills, five amendment Bills and a Supplementary Supply Bill (2026). The three new Bills are the Islamic Inheritance Bill 2026, Native Courts Bill 2026, and District Chief, Native Chief and Village Head Bill 2026. “All Bills brought before the State Legislative Assembly are important.

items.title: Businesswoman Sofia Rini to be charged with defamation today
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Businesswoman Sofia Rini Buyong is expected to be charged with defamation at the Magistrates’ Court here today. Sinar Harian reported that Bukit Aman Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar confirmed that she would be charged under Section 500 of the Penal Code. Sofia Rini was previously investigated over a video of her conversation with businessman Albert Tei that allegedly contained defamatory remarks against former Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki.

items.title: Perak launches operation after rise in wild monkey complaints at Sungai Perak R&R
items.summary: KUALA KANGSAR, July 15 — Wild monkey harassment affecting commuters at the Sungai Perak Rest and Recreation (R&R) area along the North-South Expressway is being addressed through an operation to reduce human-wildlife conflict. State Science, Environment and Green Technology Committee chairman Teh Kok Lim said the Perak Department of Wildlife and National Parks (PERHILITAN) has periodically received complaints about wild monkeys disrupting the R&R over the past few years. Teh said the operation involves cooperation among Perak PERHILITAN through its Sungai Siput and Kinta district personnel, PLUS Berhad, Edgenta Infrastructure Services (EIS), the Sungai Perak R&R management, and the Northbound Sungai Perak RSA Penghulu.

items.title: Police seize 75 bitcoin mining machines in Teluk Intan power theft raid
items.summary: IPOH, July 15 — Police seized 75 bitcoin mining machines and 12 network devices during a special operation in Teluk Intan today, where the cryptocurrency mining activities were believed to have been powered by illegally connected electricity. Hilir Perak district police chief ACP Chua Kok Lian said the operation, conducted in collaboration with Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB), successfully uncovered the illegal cryptocurrency mining activities. He said police viewed cryptocurrency mining activities involving illegal electricity connections seriously as they not only violated the law but also caused substantial losses to the utility provider and increased the risk of fires that could threaten lives and property.

items.title: Melaka PKR urges calm, seeks consensus on nominated assemblymen issue
items.summary: MELAKA, July 15 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (Keadilan) Melaka has urged all parties to not make hasty decisions that could jeopardise administrative stability, public welfare, and the state’s development. In a statement yesterday, Melaka Keadilan also urged that the issue concerning the State Constitution (Melaka) (Amendment) Enactment 2026 regarding the appointment of nominated Members of the Legislative Assembly (ADUN) be resolved in the spirit of consensus and negotiation to ensure the continued stability of the state administration. Acting Melaka Keadilan State Leadership Council (MPN) Chairman Adam Adli Abdul Halim said the party took note of the stance of five Pakatan Harapan (PH) assemblymen regarding the enactment, as well as their decision to withdraw from the state government.

items.title: In Dewan Rakyat today: Internet disruptions, West Asia conflict's impact on fuel subsidies among issues raised
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — Disruptions to internet access and the impact of the conflict in West Asia on the country’s fuel subsidy expenditure are among the key issues expected to be raised in the Dewan Rakyat today. According to the Order Paper published on Parliament’s official website, Datuk Anyi Ngau (GPS-Baram) will ask the Communications Minister to state the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission’s (MCMC) comprehensive plan to address the issue of internet services being unavailable despite mobile devices showing full signal strength. The two Bills were tabled for first reading in the Dewan Rakyat last Monday (July 13).

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Anwar warns against dragging royalty, Constitution into campaign
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, July 15 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has reminded all parties involved in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election to refrain from playing up issues concerning royal institutions and the Federal Constitution. Also present were DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, PH Communications Director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil and PH Election director Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari. Negeri Sembilan must not be left behind,” he said.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin seeks fresh Pakatan mandate to preserve stability
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, July 15 — Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has urged voters in the state to grant PH a fresh mandate in the upcoming 16th state election to ensure continued political stability and economic development. “We need to win to ensure Negeri Sembilan remains stable. Meanwhile, Anthony Loke described the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign as PH’s “second round” following the Johor state election, saying the coalition is determined to retain control of the state government.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls: Aminuddin to contest Linggi, Anthony Loke defends Chennah
items.summary: KUALA PILAH, July 15 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has been announced as Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) candidate for the Linggi seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election. Aminuddin, who is also Negeri Sembilan PH chairman, has held the Sikamat state constituency for four consecutive terms since 2008. Also present were Amanah president Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu, PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil, and the coalition’s election director, Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari.

items.title: The Johor blueprint: A winning formula or a one-state phenomenon for BN?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 15 — The landslide victory by Barisan Nasional (BN) in the July 11 state election has fuelled speculation of a broader BN comeback, but political observers caution that the “Johor template” may not be easily replicated in Negeri Sembilan’s upcoming polls. “I see it as confirmation of BN’s dominance in Johor and that the 2018 election was just a deviation. She also assessed BN’s positioning between state and federal politics.

items.title: A ride cut short: Motorcyclist killed in collision with driver who allegedly ran red light at Sungai Petani junction
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, July 14 — A motorcyclist was killed after his motorcycle crashed into a car suspected of running a red light while making a right turn at the Bandar Seri Cendana intersection here this morning. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said Wan Mohd Shamsury Wan Samsudin, 48, was pronounced dead at the scene in the 8.40 am incident when the car, driven by a woman in her 20s, was believed to have failed to stop at a red light before turning right into the path of the motorcycle. “The motorcycle, which was travelling straight, collided with the left side of the car.

items.title: Ex-civil servant under four-day MACC remand, accused of using 11 people’s details to pocket RM300,000 in aid payments
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 14 — The Kuala Lumpur Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) yesterday detained a former civil servant for allegedly submitting false documents to his workplace under a financial assistance programme involving about RM300,000. A source said the male suspect, in his 30s, was arrested around 6 pm after arriving at the Kuala Lumpur MACC office to give his statement. Kuala Lumpur MACC director Mohammad Zakkuan Talib confirmed the arrest and said the case is being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.
